brand.name: The Ballot Beacon
brand.tagline: No Slant. No Spin. Just what passed, why it matters, and how it impacts your wallet, rights.

offer.what: Subscriber-supported political newsletter that breaks down new state and federal laws in plain English — a 3-9 minute read delivered weekly to bi-weekly.
offer.coreOutcome: Understand how new laws actually affect your wallet, rights, and daily life — without partisan spin or legal jargon.
offer.pricePoint: Free tier available ('unlock free access'); paid subscription tier implied but specific price unknown.
offer.commitment: Recurring subscription (weekly/bi-weekly email cadence); self-serve signup.
leadMagnets: Free newsletter access (no paywall on entry)

audience.icp: Civically engaged adults (approx. 30-60) who are frustrated with partisan media and want factual, plain-language coverage of legislation — non-attorneys who feel shut out by legalese and exhausted by political drama.
painPoints: Can't understand how new laws actually affect their daily life until it's too late
Distrust mainstream media due to perceived partisan bias
Legal texts and legislative summaries are too technical and time-consuming
Feel uninformed and powerless about government decisions that affect them
segments: State-specific readers (TX, GA, CA, NY coverage confirmed)
Political moderates and independents who distrust both partisan sides
Working professionals who want to stay informed in 5 minutes or less
Local-news-desert readers who lack credible civic journalism in their area

salesCycle.trial: Yes — free tier with no credit card required; entire entry is free access.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve email signup; conversion to paid happens inside the newsletter or via upgrade prompt.
salesCycle.topObjection: 'Just another biased political newsletter' — credibility and partisan neutrality concern.

ahaMoment: Reading the first issue and having a real, recently passed law explained clearly in plain English, in under 5 minutes, with direct relevance to their state — without any spin.

retention.expansion: Upgrade from free reader to paid subscriber.
retention.churnRisk: Disengagement during slow news cycles; readers who feel coverage doesn't match their state; subscribers who lose trust if content is perceived as slanted.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — likely only a basic platform welcome email; no onboarding sequence to confirm the value promise and drive first-issue engagement.
Free-to-Paid Conversion — no known nurture sequence converting free readers to paid subscribers.
Renewal / Expansion — no known anniversary or upgrade nudge flow.
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no known re-engagement campaign for lapsed or inactive subscribers.
toneVoice: Plain-spoken, direct, and trustworthy. Conversational but credible — like a knowledgeable friend explaining the news, not a pundit or professor. Nonpartisan is a core identity claim. Tagline mantra: 'No Slant. No Spin. No BS.'
compliance: CAN-SPAM compliant (email newsletter). Political content — copy must avoid any language that implies partisan endorsement or could undermine the nonpartisan brand claim. No HIPAA, finance, or legal-professional-services constraints.
